title: LE manifest droid
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|LE-series}} {{DroidSeries |image=[[File:LEManifestDroid-NEGTD.png]] |name=LE manifest droid |creator= |manufacturer=[[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' |designer= |homeworld= |line= |model= |class=[[Manifest droid]] |degree=[[Class three droid/Legends|Class three droid]] |cost= |length= |width= |height=1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Feminine personality]] |sensor=Silver |plating= |armament= |equipment=*[[Vocabulator/Legends|Vocabulator]]/broadcast speaker *Magnetic footpads |affiliation= |firstmade= |retired= }} {{Quote|I am Ellie. It is my function here to note all inventories, issue clearances, and keep all records.|LE-series unit [[LE-914]]|}} The '''LE manifest droid''' was a third-degree shipping management [[Droid/Legends|droid]] produced by [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]]. The LE droid was designed to serve as a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] variant that specialized in facilitating shipping and import/export business, and found use from [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Captain/Legends|captains]] and [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] operators in the centuries before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. However, the droid's notorious honesty caused it to quickly fall out of favor—it became known for its habit of willingly reporting contraband being transported upon its ship—and the line was ultimately discontinued. One LE unit, [[LE-914]], or "Ellie," would later play a key role in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] receiving intelligence on the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star's]] construction in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. ==Description== A product of powerhouse [[Droid/Legends|droid]] manufacturers [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]], the LE manifest droid was constructed as a variant of the common [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] specifically for use by [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Captain/Legends|captains]] and [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] operators. Where most protocol droids carried etiquette programming, the LE unit was instead equipped with import/export bylaws for all [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] major trade regions and the [[Bureau of Ships and Services/Legends|Bureau of Ships and Services]]' code of conduct, which was considered too massive and intricate to realistically be fully grasped by any organic being. LE units were also programmed with a friendly, [[Gender/Legends|female]] personality, intended to make the droid an agreeable companion for long [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] journeys. LE droids were known for their strenuous honesty, which led to the droid line's cancellation, although many units that remained in service long enough ultimately learned how to make compromises when it came to the law. ==History== {{Quote|But... how did a manifest droid come to be blaster-proof?"
"Before I went into service on this world, I was privately owned. My last master had me specifically outfitted for high-stress functions...|C-3PO and LE-914|}} [[File:LE-914.jpg|thumb|left|LE-914, also known as "Ellie."]] The less-recognized cousin of the popular Cybot Galactica [[LE-series repair droid]], the LE manifest droid line began production circa [[300 BBY/Legends|300 BBY]], originally intended to perform clerical duties and expedite shipping and import/export business. However, the droid was largely a commercial failure—its scrupulous honesty became an issue in many spaceports on the fringes of the galaxy, such as those in [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt]] or [[Bothan Space]], where bribes were considered a regular part of business. LE units became known to voluntarily report contraband hidden aboard starships, and this growing reputation eventually led to the droid line's retirement. Many LE droids remained in service throughout the galaxy, however, seeing use in smaller spaceports and short-hop corporate [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]]. One LE unit—designated [[LE-914]], or "Ellie"—became notable for playing a small, but important role in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. Ellie was the personal companion of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Tay Vanis]], who had outfitted the droid with numerous modifications, including [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]-proof [[Armorplast/Legends|armorplast]] plating and an additional storage compartment that held, among other things, a [[Proton bomb/Legends|proton bomb]] with self-destruct programming. Ellie accompanied Vanis on a mission to retrieve [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]]-held intelligence on the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] construction of a [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]], only for the Imperials to chase the two of them to [[Dennaskar]]. Vanis entrusted the intelligence to Ellie, who in turn passed it on to a Rebel search team led by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] upon their arrival on Dennaskar. Ellie also helped them track down Vanis, whom they found brutally [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] in an Imperial [[Dead Castle|prison]]. Her master essentially a mind-wiped husk, Ellie activated her self-destruct, destroying herself and ending Vanis' suffering.[[Star Wars (1977) 80|''Star Wars'' (1977) 80]] ==Behind the scenes== The LE manifest droid first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 80|''Star Wars'' (1977) 80]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]] and released in [[1983]]. Illustrator [[Ron Frenz]] designed the droid to emulate [[Ralph McQuarrie]]'s original concept drawings for [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]],{{SW|url=news/back-from-the-drawing-board-part-2-repurposed-star-wars-technology|text=Repurposed ''Star Wars'' Technology - Back from the Drawing Board, Part 2|nolive=1}} which in turn was heavily influenced by the sleek, art deco style of the robot Maria from [[Wikipedia:Fritz Lang|Fritz Lang's]] classic 1927 science fiction film ''[[Wikipedia:Metropolis (1927 film)|Metropolis]]''. Finding that the LE designation was identical to that of the LE repair droid that originally appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'', ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' author [[Daniel Wallace]] opted to connect the two droid classes as products of Cybot Galactica.{{Blog|danwallace|53|Endnotes for The New Essential Guide to Droids: PART 2 of 3}} ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 80|''Star Wars'' (1977) 80]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 80}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' {{1stID|LE manifest droid}} *{{Blog|danwallace|53|Endnotes for The New Essential Guide to Droids: PART 2 of 3}} *{{SW|url=news/id-just-as-soon-kiss-a-wookiee-star-wars-romances|text=I'd Just as Soon Kiss a Wookiee: ''Star Wars'' Romances|nolive=1}} *{{SW|url=news/back-from-the-drawing-board-part-2-repurposed-star-wars-technology|text=Repurposed ''Star Wars'' Technology - Back from the Drawing Board, Part 2|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Cybot Galactica products]] [[Category:Protocol droid models]]
